tldr.text: Google's Gemma 4 12B lands as a laptop-ready open model with native audio and vision, while Anthropic launches Claude Tag to let teams @-mention Claude directly inside Slack.
Ramp nearly triples its valuation to $44 billion after raising $750 million, and Suno pulls in $400 million at a $5.4 billion valuation despite ongoing copyright lawsuits.

Congress dropped a 269-page Great American AI Act that would freeze state AI development laws for three years, setting up a showdown with California, Colorado, and every other state that has moved first.

categories.name: Tools & Products
categories.blurb: From laptop-friendly open models to AI teammates living inside your Slack, the agentic era is arriving on every surface.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B runs on your laptop — and listens
stories.summary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, an open Apache 2.0 model that fits in 16 GB of RAM and is the first mid-sized Gemma to accept native audio input alongside vision and text. It ditches traditional multimodal encoders entirely, piping all inputs straight into the LLM backbone for cleaner reasoning and lower latency. Benchmark performance reportedly approaches the much larger 26B MoE model, making it a compelling local option for agentic workflows.

stories.title: Claude joins your Slack as a real teammate
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a beta feature letting Enterprise and Team customers add Claude to Slack channels where anyone can @-mention it, delegate multi-step tasks, and watch it work asynchronously over hours or days. Claude builds shared context across a channel, takes initiative when ambient mode is on, and respects per-org spend limits set by admins. It runs on Opus 4.8 and replaces the older Claude Slack app.

stories.title: ChatGPT gets personal finance, charts, and a new default model
stories.summary: OpenAI's latest release notes bundle several meaningful upgrades: a personal finance experience rolling out to Plus users, interactive charts that appear inline in conversations, and GPT-5.5 Instant replacing GPT-5.3 Instant as the default model for all users. Full-screen writing blocks now support essays, PRDs, and long-form docs saved to a Library, and memory capacity for paid users has doubled. The company also set sunset dates — GPT-4.5 retires June 26 and o3 retires August 26.

stories.title: Kaggle lets AI agents write their own benchmarks
stories.summary: Google's Kaggle team launched local development support for Kaggle Benchmarks, meaning developers can now create, validate, push, and run evaluation tasks from their own environment — VS Code, Cursor, or any coding agent — instead of the web-only notebook editor. A new write-kaggle-benchmarks skill lets an agent build a full benchmark task from a plain-language description. The update integrates with Google's Antigravity platform and the Kaggle CLI.

stories.title: Gemini Omni and the agentic everything tour
stories.summary: Google's May recap confirmed Gemini Omni — a model that takes any combination of image, audio, video, and text as input and generates high-quality video grounded in real-world knowledge. The same update introduced Universal Cart for cross-merchant shopping inside Search and YouTube, Android Halo for managing agents in the background, and the Googlebook laptop built around Gemini Intelligence. Gemini 3.5 Pro is expected to follow in June.

categories.name: Startups & Money
categories.blurb: Corporate fintech, AI music, and Indian language models are all minting unicorns this week as investors chase anything with an AI story.

stories.title: Ramp hits $44B — nearly triple last year's price
stories.summary: Corporate expense platform Ramp raised $750 million at a $44 billion valuation, led by ICONIQ, GIC, and Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, D.E. Shaw, and Peter Thiel's Founders Fund also writing checks. The round brings Ramp's total funding past $3 billion and reflects surging appetite for fintech companies that can credibly attach an AI narrative to their core product. The valuation is nearly three times where Ramp stood just a year ago.

stories.title: Suno raises $400M while lawyers circle
stories.summary: AI music generator Suno closed a $400 million round at a $5.4 billion valuation, led by Bond Capital alongside IVP, Forerunner, Union Square Ventures, Lightspeed, and Menlo Ventures. The raise comes despite the startup facing multiple active copyright lawsuits from major labels over its training data. Investors appear to be betting that the market opportunity outweighs the legal risk.

stories.title: Sarvam is India's newest AI unicorn
stories.summary: Indian AI startup Sarvam raised $234 million at a $1.5 billion valuation in a Series B led by HCLTech, with Bessemer, Khosla Ventures, and Peak XV also participating. The company, known for open-source language models designed for Indian languages at 30B and 105B parameters, is targeting a total raise of $300 million for the round. The deal marks a significant milestone for India's domestic AI ecosystem.

stories.title: PhysicsX hits $2.4B making AI for jet engines
stories.summary: London-based PhysicsX, which builds AI simulation models for manufacturing components including jet engines and semiconductors, raised $300 million at roughly a $2.4 billion valuation in a Series C led by Singapore's Temasek. Nvidia and Applied Materials are existing backers, and the company's valuation has more than doubled since its last round. PhysicsX competes in a field that now includes ventures backed by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.

categories.name: The Bigger Picture
categories.blurb: Congress wants to freeze state AI laws for three years, Trump signed an AI security executive order, and a federal court just ruled that AI prompts used by expert witnesses can be subpoenaed.

stories.title: Great American AI Act: one bill to preempt them all
stories.summary: Reps. Jay Obernolte and Lori Trahan dropped a 269-page bipartisan discussion draft of the Great American AI Act, the most sweeping federal AI governance proposal to date. Its headline provision would preempt all state AI development regulations — including California's suite of bills and Colorado's Act scheduled to take effect June 30 — for three years. Companies with over $500 million in revenue would have to publish Frontier AI Frameworks, report safety incidents, submit to audits, and fund a $100 million federal AI standards center.

stories.title: Trump's AI executive order: voluntary, not mandatory
stories.summary: President Trump signed the executive order 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security' on June 2, prioritizing U.S. AI leadership through private-sector collaboration rather than regulatory mandates. The order explicitly rules out any licensing, preclearance, or permitting requirements for AI model development, and instead establishes voluntary frameworks and cybersecurity benchmarks for frontier models. It also accelerates AI adoption inside national security agencies via NSPM-11.

stories.title: AI prompts are now fair game in court
stories.summary: A federal magistrate judge in Conservation Law Foundation v. Shell Oil ruled that the AI prompts an expert witness used to prepare her report must be produced in discovery, treating them as part of her methodology rather than protected draft material. The decision is one of the clearest signals yet that courts will not grant AI-assisted expert work special protection. It adds momentum to a growing body of case law treating chatbot records as evidence.

stories.title: UK's CMA forces Google to let publishers opt out of AI search
stories.summary: The UK Competition and Markets Authority imposed new conduct requirements on Google Search, giving publishers the right to opt out of having their content used in AI Overviews and AI model fine-tuning. Google must also provide clear attribution and links within AI-generated results. The CMA said it will monitor compliance and consider further intervention if necessary.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI over ChatGPT safety concealment
stories.summary: Florida's Attorney General filed a civil lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging the company knowingly hid serious risks of ChatGPT from consumers including children and actively marketed the product to Florida residents despite those risks. The complaint cites deceptive trade practices, responses that could promote self-harm, dependency-inducing design, and unauthorized data collection from minors. The case is one of the first state-led civil actions framing AI safety as a consumer protection issue.
